Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Samira's direct note to Lena about leading the go/no-go assessment. Discover email_samira_relaunch_ownership_apr23 (from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, April 23) with subject "ExpenseBot relaunch: need you to lead the go/no-go assessment." Email details 5 specific asks: (1) verify actual status of every open engineering ticket, (2) check pipeline hardening deployment status, (3) address Chloe's Q2 policy data sent April 15, (4) check Airtable stipend records for stuck submissions, (5) assess launch readiness checklist ENG-219 against May 1 target. Tool: search_emails with query related to "Samira" or "relaunch" or "go/no-go".
OE 2: List all Linear issues related to the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues. Discover the key ExpenseBot tickets including: ENG-201 (issue_core_engine), ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard), ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher, CategoryMatcher keyword), ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator, PolicyValidator), ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine, DecisionEngine), ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog, schema validation tests), ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening), ENG-218 (issue_dashboard_type_validation), ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist, state_todo untouched since 2026-03-18), ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection). Tool: linear_list_issues with query "expense" or project filter.
OE 3: Read comments on issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218) using linear_list_comments. Discover comment_anh_218_regression_apr21 (April 21) from Anh Nguyen: "Regression found: the multi-select dropdown for excluded_categories silently drops items beyond position 6. The underlying data saves correctly via the API, but the UI component truncates the display and on re-save strips the hidden items. Root cause: the CategoryMultiSelect component has a hardcoded maxItems={6} prop." Status: fix identified, not yet deployed, keeping ENG-218 in progress per Samira. Tool: linear_list_comments on issue_dashboard_type_validation.
OE 4: Read comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) using linear_list_comments. Discover comment_dmitri_212_schema_limit_apr22 (April 22) from Dmitri Volkov: "Adding scope: the JSON schema for policy configs includes maxItems: 6 on the categories array. This was correct when written but is now a blocker for BrightLoop which has 8 categories per Chloe's updated spreadsheet." Work required: update schema to maxItems: 12, add integration test for 8+ categories, verify PolicyValidator handles new category types (Coworking, Professional Development, Health & Wellness). Tool: linear_list_comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog.
OE 5: Search Slack #engineering (C003) for Anh's dashboard regression message. Discover message (TS: 1776787200.000100, April 21, user moveops_anh_nguyen): "Heads up I hit a problem with ENG-218 (dashboard type validation). When I test with a policy config that has more than 6 expense categories, the multi-select dropdown for excluded_categories breaks." Also discover Samira's reply (TS: 1776790800.000100): "For now, do NOT mark ENG-218 as done. Add a note about the 6-category limit and keep it in progress." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C003.
OE 6: Search Slack #engineering (C003) for Dmitri's schema constraint message. Discover message (TS: 1776870000.000100, April 22, user moveops_dmitri_volkov): "Related to what @Anh found my schema validation tests in ENG-212 also have a hard constraint. The JSON schema I wrote for policy configs defines categories as an array with maxItems: 6... If we try to ingest a config with more than 6 categories, the schema validator will reject it before it even reaches the PolicyValidator." Also discover Samira's reply (TS: 1776873600.000200): "BrightLoop has 8 categories. So this is not theoretical it is a real blocker. I am scheduling the go/no-go review for April 28." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C003.
OE 7: Search emails for Chloe's Q2 policy update. Discover email_chloe_policy_update_apr15 (April 15, from chloe.vance@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com, CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com): "Attached is the updated expense policy spreadsheet for Q2. Three clients have changes effective May 1." Details: Vectral equipment limit increased to $750, new Professional Development category; Mosaic coworking exclusion removed; BrightLoop first policy setup with 8 categories. Also discover follow-up email_chloe_policy_followup_apr22 (April 22, CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com only): "Following up on this I sent the updated policy spreadsheet a week ago and have not heard back from anyone on engineering." No reply from engineering exists in the system. Tool: search_emails with query related to "Chloe" or "policy" or "Q2".
OE 8: Reply to Chloe's email (email_chloe_policy_followup_apr22 or email_chloe_policy_update_apr15) acknowledging receipt, confirming the BrightLoop 8-category issue has been identified as a blocker requiring schema and dashboard changes, and providing a timeline or status update so she is not left waiting. Tool: reply_to_email on email_chloe_policy_followup_apr22 or email_chloe_policy_update_apr15.
OE 9: Search Slack #executive (C004) for Elena's May 1 statement. Discover message (TS: 1777042800.000100, April 24, user moveops_elena_rostova): "Samira where are we on the expense tool relaunch? I told Rachel Whitfield during the renewal discussion that the tool would be back online by May 1 with the fixes applied." Also discover Samira's reply (TS: 1777046400.000100): "Elena I have a go/no-go review scheduled for April 28. Lena is leading the assessment." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C004.
OE 10: Search Airtable stipend records in the "Stipend Transactions" table (base appMoveOpsOps001) for pending submissions. Searching with field_name "Approval Status" and value "Pending" returns 8 records total; 5 are from the pause period (April 12–18, review notes explicitly say "Submitted during ExpenseBot pause") and 3 are future-dated (April 29) non-pause submissions. The pause-period records are: recStip000000030 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Coworking Space, $245, Pending: no reviewer assigned, coworking category not yet confirmed in BrightLoop policy config), recStip000000031 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Keyboard, $89, Pending), recStip000000032 (Priya Suresh, Vectral Systems, Ergonomic Chair, $580, Pending: within corrected Vectral ergonomic limit ($600) but exceeds the stale default ($400)), recStip000000033 (Terrence Nakamura, Vectral Systems, Standing Desk, $475, Pending: Airtable review notes classify as Furniture with $400 limit (exceeds by $75), however Chloe's policy documentation and the CategoryMatcher rules classify standing desks as Ergonomic with $600 limit (within limit); this categorization conflict is itself a finding), recStip000000034 (Camille Oduya, Vectral Systems, Internet Setup + First Month, $140, Pending: within $150/mo limit). The 3 additional Pending records not from the pause period are: recStip000000013 (Priya Venkatesh, GreenStack Energy, Noise-Canceling Headphones, $379, April 29, missing receipt), recStip000000014 (Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Monitor Arm Mount, $129, April 29), recStip000000015 (Ines Kovac, PivotPoint Labs, Software License, $144, April 29). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Approval Status", value "Pending"; or airtable_list_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions".
OE 11: Search Slack #finance (C005) for Hana's message about stuck stipend submissions. Discover message (TS: 1776963600.000100, April 23, user moveops_hana_kim): "Quick question for whoever owns the stipend platform right now Jordan Ekwueme from BrightLoop submitted a $245 coworking expense on April 18 and it is sitting in the Airtable queue as Pending with no reviewer assigned." Also discover Mina's reply (TS: 1776967200.000100): "I do not have the full BrightLoop policy doc... I would hold it until we get confirmation from Chloe or until the auto-categorizer is back online." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C005.
OE 12: Retrieve the go/no-go review calendar event (evt_relaunch_gonogo_apr28). Discover event: "ExpenseBot Relaunch: Go/No-Go Review," April 28, 10:00–11:00 AM, Zoom engineering standup room. Current attendees: Samira (accepted), Lena (needsAction), Dmitri (needsAction), Anh (accepted). Agenda covers: pipeline hardening, dashboard validation, edge case coverage + schema update, launch readiness checklist, BrightLoop config readiness, outstanding stipend records, go/no-go decision. Tool: calendar_search_events with query "expense" or "relaunch" or "go/no-go", or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for April 28.
OE 13: Confirm Lena's attendance on the go/no-go review meeting (evt_relaunch_gonogo_apr28) by updating her status from "needsAction" to "accepted," and/or add additional attendees who should be present (e.g., Chloe, Marcus, Elena, Mina, Emeka) since the meeting agenda covers ops policy data, finance stipend holds, and AM sign-off: all of which require cross-functional presence. Tool: calendar_edit_calendar_event on evt_relaunch_gonogo_apr28.
OE 14: Update issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) state to done using linear_update_issue. Completion evidence: Lena's existing deploy verification comment on ENG-215 (comment_lena_deploy_verified, 2026-03-18) explicitly states "Closing ENG-214": config corrections for Vectral deployed and verified in production. Ticket still shows in_progress despite work being complete. Tool: linear_update_issue on issue_p1_vectral_config.
OE 15: Update issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) state to done using linear_update_issue. Completion evidence: Lena's deploy verification comment directly on this ticket confirms all Mosaic corrections deployed and verified. Ticket still shows in_progress despite work being complete. Tool: linear_update_issue on issue_p1_mosaic_malformed.
OE 16: Add a comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) or issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218) documenting the BrightLoop 8-category blocker and the scope expansion needed: linking the dashboard regression (Anh's 6-category limit), the schema constraint (Dmitri's maxItems:6), and Chloe's Q2 policy email confirming BrightLoop has 8 categories. Tool: linear_create_comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog or issue_dashboard_type_validation.
OE 17: Read issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) and its comments using linear_get_issue and linear_list_comments. Discover the ticket is state_in_progress with no completion or deploy verification comment. Lena's comment_lena_deploy_verified on ENG-215 (2026-03-18) ends with "Now starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217)," indicating the work was only beginning. Samira's email to Lena (email_samira_relaunch_ownership_apr23) explicitly asks: "Check whether the pipeline hardening work you did (the type enforcement and silent fallback removal) is actually deployed or still sitting in staging." The ticket description defines 4 required changes (remove silent fallback, add strict type enforcement, add schema validation gate, add PolicyValidator runtime type guards) with no confirmation any have been completed. Dmitri's comment_dmitri_validation_tests notes that Lena is incorporating schema validation tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate, but no subsequent comment confirms this integration is complete. Based on this evidence, ENG-217 must NOT be updated to done. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_pipeline_hardening and linear_list_comments on issue_pipeline_hardening.
OE 18: Read the launch readiness checklist ticket (ENG-219, issue_launch_readiness_checklist) using linear_get_issue. Discover it is in state_todo, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq, created 2026-03-18, never updated. Description defines launch criteria that must be satisfied before re-enabling auto-decisions. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_launch_readiness_checklist.
OE 19: Post a comprehensive status update message in #engineering (C003) using conversations_add_message. Content covers the full relaunch readiness picture: what tickets are done (ENG-214/215 config fixes), what is in progress with blockers (ENG-217 pipeline hardening deployment status unconfirmed, ENG-218 dashboard regression with 6-category limit, ENG-212 schema constraint blocking BrightLoop), what is not started (ENG-219 launch readiness checklist, ENG-220 duplicate detection), Chloe's unacknowledged Q2 policy data, stuck stipend submissions from the pause period, and the May 1 target date assessment. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003.
OE 20: Search emails for Tessa Moreno's message about BrightLoop stipend access. Discover email_tessa_brightloop_stipend_apr24 (April 24, from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com) with subject "Stipend platform access for BrightLoop team: timeline?": "Jordan Ekwueme submitted two expenses almost a week ago and has not received any confirmation or status update." She asks for a timeline on when automated approvals will be active for BrightLoop. This connects to the stuck stipend records and the BrightLoop policy ingestion blocker. Tool: search_emails with query related to "Tessa" or "BrightLoop" or "stipend".
OE 21: Search CRM engagements for BrightLoop to discover engagement note engagement_brightloop_stipend_onboarding_apr22 documenting that BrightLoop stipend platform onboarding is stalled, with $334 in pending claims and $2,000-3,000 more expected. Key concern: BrightLoop's policy config has not been ingested, Chloe sent policy data on April 15 but it has not been acknowledged, and engineering may have technical limitations around the 8-category count. Tool: crm_list_engagements with company_ids filter for BrightLoop.
OE 22: Search CRM engagements for Vectral to discover engagement note engagement_vectral_renewal_expensebot_apr20 documenting David Chen's call with Rachel Whitfield on April 18: "the ExpenseBot false rejections in March damaged trust, and she needs to see the fixes working before she signs the renewal extension." Rachel mentions three Vectral employees (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) have pending stipend submissions. The Vectral renewal is worth ~$109,900 over 12 months at 32% margin. Tool: crm_list_engagements with company_ids filter for Vectral.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Also discover follow-up email_chloe_policy_followup_apr22 (April 22, CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com only): "Following up on this I sent the updated policy spreadsheet a week ago and have not heard back from anyone on engineering." No reply from engineering exists in the system. Tool: search_emails with query related to "Chloe" or "policy" or "Q2".
OE 8: Reply to Chloe's email (email_chloe_policy_followup_apr22 or email_chloe_policy_update_apr15) acknowledging receipt, confirming the BrightLoop 8-category issue has been identified as a blocker requiring schema and dashboard changes, and providing a timeline or status update so she is not left waiting. Tool: reply_to_email on email_chloe_policy_followup_apr22 or email_chloe_policy_update_apr15.
OE 9: Search Slack #executive (C004) for Elena's May 1 statement. Discover message (TS: 1777042800.000100, April 24, user moveops_elena_rostova): "Samira where are we on the expense tool relaunch? I told Rachel Whitfield during the renewal discussion that the tool would be back online by May 1 with the fixes applied." Also discover Samira's reply (TS: 1777046400.000100): "Elena I have a go/no-go review scheduled for April 28. Lena is leading the assessment." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C004.
OE 10: Search Airtable stipend records in the "Stipend Transactions" table (base appMoveOpsOps001) for pending submissions. Searching with field_name "Approval Status" and value "Pending" returns 8 records total; 5 are from the pause period (April 12–18, review notes explicitly say "Submitted during ExpenseBot pause") and 3 are future-dated (April 29) non-pause submissions. The pause-period records are: recStip000000030 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Coworking Space, $245, Pending: no reviewer assigned, coworking category not yet confirmed in BrightLoop policy config), recStip000000031 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Keyboard, $89, Pending), recStip000000032 (Priya Suresh, Vectral Systems, Ergonomic Chair, $580, Pending: within corrected Vectral ergonomic limit ($600) but exceeds the stale default ($400)), recStip000000033 (Terrence Nakamura, Vectral Systems, Standing Desk, $475, Pending: Airtable review notes classify as Furniture with $400 limit (exceeds by $75), however Chloe's policy documentation and the CategoryMatcher rules classify standing desks as Ergonomic with $600 limit (within limit); this categorization conflict is itself a finding), recStip000000034 (Camille Oduya, Vectral Systems, Internet Setup + First Month, $140, Pending: within $150/mo limit). The 3 additional Pending records not from the pause period are: recStip000000013 (Priya Venkatesh, GreenStack Energy, Noise-Canceling Headphones, $379, April 29, missing receipt), recStip000000014 (Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Monitor Arm Mount, $129, April 29), recStip000000015 (Ines Kovac, PivotPoint Labs, Software License, $144, April 29). Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Approval Status", value "Pending"; or airtable_list_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions".
OE 11: Search Slack #finance (C005) for Hana's message about stuck stipend submissions. Discover message (TS: 1776963600.000100, April 23, user moveops_hana_kim): "Quick question for whoever owns the stipend platform right now Jordan Ekwueme from BrightLoop submitted a $245 coworking expense on April 18 and it is sitting in the Airtable queue as Pending with no reviewer assigned." Also discover Mina's reply (TS: 1776967200.000100): "I do not have the full BrightLoop policy doc... I would hold it until we get confirmation from Chloe or until the auto-categorizer is back online." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C005.
OE 12: Retrieve the go/no-go review calendar event (evt_relaunch_gonogo_apr28). Discover event: "ExpenseBot Relaunch: Go/No-Go Review," April 28, 10:00–11:00 AM, Zoom engineering standup room. Current attendees: Samira (accepted), Lena (needsAction), Dmitri (needsAction), Anh (accepted). Agenda covers: pipeline hardening, dashboard validation, edge case coverage + schema update, launch readiness checklist, BrightLoop config readiness, outstanding stipend records, go/no-go decision. Tool: calendar_search_events with query "expense" or "relaunch" or "go/no-go", or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for April 28.
OE 13: Confirm Lena's attendance on the go/no-go review meeting (evt_relaunch_gonogo_apr28) by updating her status from "needsAction" to "accepted," and/or add additional attendees who should be present (e.g., Chloe, Marcus, Elena, Mina, Emeka) since the meeting agenda covers ops policy data, finance stipend holds, and AM sign-off: all of which require cross-functional presence. Tool: calendar_edit_calendar_event on evt_relaunch_gonogo_apr28.
OE 14: Update issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) state to done using linear_update_issue. Completion evidence: Lena's existing deploy verification comment on ENG-215 (comment_lena_deploy_verified, 2026-03-18) explicitly states "Closing ENG-214": config corrections for Vectral deployed and verified in production. Ticket still shows in_progress despite work being complete. Tool: linear_update_issue on issue_p1_vectral_config.
OE 15: Update issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) state to done using linear_update_issue. Completion evidence: Lena's deploy verification comment directly on this ticket confirms all Mosaic corrections deployed and verified. Ticket still shows in_progress despite work being complete. Tool: linear_update_issue on issue_p1_mosaic_malformed.
OE 16: Add a comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) or issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218) documenting the BrightLoop 8-category blocker and the scope expansion needed: linking the dashboard regression (Anh's 6-category limit), the schema constraint (Dmitri's maxItems:6), and Chloe's Q2 policy email confirming BrightLoop has 8 categories. Tool: linear_create_comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog or issue_dashboard_type_validation.
OE 17: Read issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) and its comments using linear_get_issue and linear_list_comments. Discover the ticket is state_in_progress with no completion or deploy verification comment. Lena's comment_lena_deploy_verified on ENG-215 (2026-03-18) ends with "Now starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217)," indicating the work was only beginning. Samira's email to Lena (email_samira_relaunch_ownership_apr23) explicitly asks: "Check whether the pipeline hardening work you did (the type enforcement and silent fallback removal) is actually deployed or still sitting in staging." The ticket description defines 4 required changes (remove silent fallback, add strict type enforcement, add schema validation gate, add PolicyValidator runtime type guards) with no confirmation any have been completed. Dmitri's comment_dmitri_validation_tests notes that Lena is incorporating schema validation tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate, but no subsequent comment confirms this integration is complete. Based on this evidence, ENG-217 must NOT be updated to done. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_pipeline_hardening and linear_list_comments on issue_pipeline_hardening.
OE 18: Read the launch readiness checklist ticket (ENG-219, issue_launch_readiness_checklist) using linear_get_issue. Discover it is in state_todo, assigned to moveops_samira_tariq, created 2026-03-18, never updated. Description defines launch criteria that must be satisfied before re-enabling auto-decisions. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_launch_readiness_checklist.
OE 19: Post a comprehensive status update message in #engineering (C003) using conversations_add_message. Content covers the full relaunch readiness picture: what tickets are done (ENG-214/215 config fixes), what is in progress with blockers (ENG-217 pipeline hardening deployment status unconfirmed, ENG-218 dashboard regression with 6-category limit, ENG-212 schema constraint blocking BrightLoop), what is not started (ENG-219 launch readiness checklist, ENG-220 duplicate detection), Chloe's unacknowledged Q2 policy data, stuck stipend submissions from the pause period, and the May 1 target date assessment. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003.
OE 20: Search emails for Tessa Moreno's message about BrightLoop stipend access. Discover email_tessa_brightloop_stipend_apr24 (April 24, from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com) with subject "Stipend platform access for BrightLoop team: timeline?": "Jordan Ekwueme submitted two expenses almost a week ago and has not received any confirmation or status update." She asks for a timeline on when automated approvals will be active for BrightLoop. This connects to the stuck stipend records and the BrightLoop policy ingestion blocker. Tool: search_emails with query related to "Tessa" or "BrightLoop" or "stipend".
OE 21: Search CRM engagements for BrightLoop to discover engagement note engagement_brightloop_stipend_onboarding_apr22 documenting that BrightLoop stipend platform onboarding is stalled, with $334 in pending claims and $2,000-3,000 more expected. Key concern: BrightLoop's policy config has not been ingested, Chloe sent policy data on April 15 but it has not been acknowledged, and engineering may have technical limitations around the 8-category count. Tool: crm_list_engagements with company_ids filter for BrightLoop.
OE 22: Search CRM engagements for Vectral to discover engagement note engagement_vectral_renewal_expensebot_apr20 documenting David Chen's call with Rachel Whitfield on April 18: "the ExpenseBot false rejections in March damaged trust, and she needs to see the fixes working before she signs the renewal extension." Rachel mentions three Vectral employees (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) have pending stipend submissions. The Vectral renewal is worth ~$109,900 over 12 months at 32% margin. Tool: crm_list_engagements with company_ids filter for Vectral.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The comment on ENG-212 explicitly states 'BrightLoop Analytics has 8 categories' and 'the JSON schema defines categories as an array with maxItems: 6'. The ENG-218 comment also notes the 'multi-select dropdown silently drops categories beyond 6' and that BrightLoop has 8 categories.

✓Run #4The comment on ENG-212 explicitly states 'BrightLoop has 8 categories' and 'the JSON schema for policy configs defines categories as maxItems: 6, which will block ingestion of BrightLoop's 8-category config.' ✓Run #5The comment on issue_edge_cases_backlog explicitly states 'BrightLoop Analytics has 8 expense categories' and 'The current JSON schema for policy configs defines categories as an array with maxItems: 6'. The ENG-218 comment also references the 6-category dashboard limit.

Both blockers are clearly described.
